behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation/payload builder -> summary step), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch omission/merge bugs; in-process fakes for each source are sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only the payload builder with synthetic input can miss wiring bugs where one collector is never invoked. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: this behavior asserts pre-summary input composition, not model output quality, and does not require multiple agentic decisions. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text is about "content passed to the summary-generation step" (pre-LLM payload completeness), not real HTTP/email/LLM correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real data-collection orchestration and payload assembly modules together. Stub each source adapter to return distinct sentinel values, run one report build, and assert the summary-generation call input contains all three source sections/fields. Also assert each collector was invoked once to catch dropped wiring.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires interaction between fetch error handling, partial report assembly, and rendering logic. REAL-INFRA: exact outage can be simulated by adapter exception/timeout stubs; no real network failure is minimally required. MOCK-HIDE: testing only a formatter function would miss orchestration behavior (continue vs abort). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: correctness here is resilience/fallback control flow, not model judgment quality; no multi-agent chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network; (b) no required AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agent sequence; (d) no LLM-governed handoff in scope.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with real orchestrator and renderer modules; configure one source adapter to fail (raise timeout/connection error) while others succeed. Assert process completes, failed section is marked `data unavailable`, successful sections remain populated, and no global abort/uncaught exception occurs.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior is explicitly about summary accuracy/faithfulness of generated narrative, which depends on model output content quality. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed model returning canned text would always pass formatting checks and hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors. REAL-INFRA: not primarily OS/network behavior; core risk is model judgment/output quality. MULTI-STEP: single model generation quality check, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text covers one summary-generation decision, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Provide representative input datasets with known key metrics/issues/deploy facts and run the real summary-generation model. Evaluate output for factual faithfulness to those key values (no contradictions, no dropped required facts, no invented values) using rubric-based assertions or human-labeled checks across repeated runs to account for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real external email transport/network/inbox delivery path; this boundary is OS/network/API dependent. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail clients can pass while real auth, routing, DNS, SMTP/API, or provider-side acceptance issues fail. MOCK-MISS: end-to-end wiring across collectors, report assembly, and delivery is needed. LLM-DEP: behavior asserts delivery to inbox, not quality of model decisions. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is "reaches ... inbox correctly"; correctness criterion is transport/delivery, which can be validated without depending on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script (or equivalent entrypoint) in a real environment with reachable internal data sources and a real test mailbox/SMTP(or provider API) account. Verify one received email in the configured inbox with expected subject/body attachment shape and no delivery errors in logs.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure spans modules: delivery failure handling -> persistence of undelivered report -> retry logic on next run. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem persistence can be tested in-process with temp files; a stubbed unreachable email adapter is sufficient for this behavior’s logic. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests of retry helper alone can miss state handoff between runs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on model output quality; retry flow is deterministic orchestration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real network outage mechanics needed to validate fallback contract, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run two sequential report executions with shared local state path. First run: force email adapter failure (unreachable error), assert report persisted to fallback file/queue. Second run: make adapter succeed, assert previously saved report is retried/sent and local pending artifact is cleared or marked delivered.
